author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
gurudeve, vraja-vane, vraja-bhūmi-vāsī jane,
śuddhabhakte, āra vipragaṇe
iṣṭamantre, hari-nāme, jugala⋅bhajana⋅kāme,
kara rati apūrva jatane

dhari, mana, caraṇe tomāra
jāniyāchi ebe sāra, kṛṣṇa-bhakti vinā āra,
nāhi ghuce jīvera saṁsāra

karma jñāna tapaḥ joga, sakaloi ta’ karma-bhoga,
karma chāḍāite keha nāre
sakala chāḍiyā bhāi, śraddhā-devīra guṇa gāi,
jā̐’ra kṛpā bhakti dite pāre

chāḍi’ dambha anukṣaṇa, smara aṣṭatattva, mana,
kara tāhe niṣkapaṭa rati
sei rati⋅prārthanāya, śrī⋅dāsagosvāmi⋅pāya,
e bhaktivinoda kare nati
en_translation:
To Gurudeva, in forests of Vraja, to the residents of Vraja, to pure devotees, and to the brāhmaṇas, With your chosen mantra, holy names of Hari, and with the desire for devotional service to the Divine Couple, apply unprecedented care and affection.

Holding, O mind, to your feet, I have now understood the essence: without Kṛṣṇa bhakti, the cycle of birth and death of the living entity cannot cease.

Fruitive action, knowledge, austerity, yoga - all are indeed reactions of karma. No one can escape karma. Leaving all else, O brother, I sing the glories of the goddess of faith, by whose mercy devotion can be given.

Constantly abandoning pride, O mind, remember the eight principles and render sincere affection unto them. With that prayer for affection, this Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura offers obeisances at the feet of Śrī Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmī.
